perturbation.original_text: Zogenix hereby appoints Distributor, and Distributor accepts appointment, as the exclusive distributor (even as to Zogenix) of the Product in the Field in the Territory during the Term, and grants to Distributor the exclusive rights to maintain Regulatory Approval of (while Distributor is the MAH Party), package, promote, market, offer for sale, sell, import and distribute the Product in the Field in the Territory during the Term.
perturbation.changed_text: Zogenix hereby appoints Distributor, and Distributor accepts appointment, as the exclusive vendor (even as to Zogenix) of the Product in the Field in the Territory during the Term, and grants to Distributor the exclusive rights to maintain Regulatory Approval of (while Distributor is the MAH Party), package, promote, market, offer for sale, sell, import and distribute the Product in the Field in the Territory during the Term.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'distributor' to 'vendor' in Section 2.1 introduces confusion. The original text uses 'Distributor' consistently throughout the agreement to define the entity responsible for distribution. Changing it to 'vendor' here, without defining vendor, creates uncertainty about whether 'vendor' and 'Distributor' are the same, impacting the interpretation of rights and obligations. This contradicts with all sections that are being mentioned to Distributor.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Grant of Rights to Distributor. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Zogenix hereby appoints Distributor, and Distributor accepts appointment, as the exclusive distributor (even as to Zogenix) of the Product in the Field in the Territory during the Term, and grants to Distributor the exclusive rights to maintain Regulatory Approval of (while Distributor is the MAH Party), package, promote, market, offer for sale, sell, import and distribute the Product in the Field in the Territory during the Term. The Parties agree that the rights granted in this Section 2.1 do not grant any right or license to, and Distributor shall not, supply, sell, offer for sale or otherwise dispose of or provide access to the Product in the Territory unless and until the Product obtains Regulatory Approval in the Field in the Territory.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'distributor' to 'vendor' in the changed text introduces a Misaligned Terminology, as defined in the Perturbation Definitions. While both terms relate to the supply chain, 'distributor' is specifically defined and used consistently throughout the original agreement. The contradicted text also uses the term 'Distributor'. Changing to 'vendor' without proper definition creates ambiguity, potentially altering the rights and obligations initially assigned to 'Distributor'. This creates an in-text contradiction because the contract relies on the clear and consistent meaning of 'Distributor' in other clauses.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor shall provide updates regularly to the JSC relating to Commercialization activities for the Product in the Field in the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: Vendor shall provide updates regularly to the JSC relating to Commercialization activities for the Product in the Field in the Territory.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Distributor' to 'Vendor' in Section 5.1(a) creates an in-text contradiction. The agreement consistently refers to 'Distributor' for commercialization responsibilities. Introducing 'Vendor' here causes uncertainty about which entity is obligated to provide updates, especially because in section 2.1 the term Vendor was introduced. This contradicts with all sections that are being mentioned to Distributor.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distributor Responsibilities. Distributor shall have the exclusive right to market, promote, sell, offer for sale, import, package and otherwise Commercialize the Product in the Field in the Territory, at its sole cost and expense, in accordance with Applicable Laws and the Commercialization Plan and subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement. Without limiting the foregoing, Distributor will have the exclusive right and responsibility in the Field in the Territory for the following: (i) designing the Commercialization strategy and tactics for the Product, subject to JSC approval of the Commercialization Plan; (ii) undertaking all promotional activities for the Product; (iii) establishing and implementing post-marketing surveillance studies for the Product in the Territory as required or recommended by a Regulatory Authority; (iv) receiving, accepting and filling orders for the Product from customers; (v) warehousing and distributing the Product to customers; (vi) controlling invoicing, order processing and collection of accounts receivable for sales of the Product; (vii) recording sales of the Product in the Territory in its books of account for sales; and (viii) providing results of sales of the Product in the Territory for purposes of periodic safety reports and exposure estimates. Vendor shall provide updates regularly to the JSC relating to Commercialization activities for the Product in the Field in the Territory.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Distributor' with 'Vendor' in Section 5.1(a). The contradicted text explicitly outlines the 'Distributor Responsibilities,' including commercialization activities. By introducing 'Vendor' in the changed text, a direct conflict arises regarding which entity is responsible for providing updates on commercialization activities. This contradicts the Distributor's explicitly stated responsibilities, creating uncertainty and potential unenforceability as it's unclear whether the Distributor or Vendor (or both) is/are obligated to provide these updates. Since section 2.1 introduces the term Vendor, the change is introducing a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor shall comply with reasonable policies provided by Zogenix from time to time to maintain the goodwill and value of the Zogenix Trademarks.
perturbation.changed_text: Vendor shall comply with reasonable policies provided by Zogenix from time to time to maintain the goodwill and value of the Zogenix Trademarks.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Distributor' to 'Vendor' in Section 9.7 creates an in-text contradiction and leads to uncertainty in enforcement. The agreement consistently refers to 'Distributor' for all Trademark related matters and responsibilities. Introducing 'Vendor' here causes uncertainty about which entity is obligated to comply with policies and also protect the trademarks, as in section 2.1 the term Vendor was introduced. This contradicts with all sections that are being mentioned to Distributor.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Zogenix Trademarks. All packaging, promotional materials, package inserts, and labeling for the Product in the Field in the Territory shall bear, free of charge, one or more house Trademarks chosen and owned by Zogenix, including the Zogenix name and logo set forth in Exhibit 9.7 (each, a "Zogenix Trademark"). Zogenix or its Affiliates shall own all right, title, and interest in and to all Zogenix Trademarks, all corresponding trademark applications and registrations thereof, and all common law rights thereto. Zogenix shall, at Zogenix's sole expense, have sole control over the registration, prosecution, maintenance, enforcement and defense of the Zogenix Trademarks. All goodwill of the business associated with or symbolized by the Zogenix Trademarks shall inure to the benefit of Zogenix. Distributor acknowledges Zogenix's exclusive ownership of the Zogenix Trademarks and agrees not to take any action inconsistent with such ownership. Distributor shall provide Zogenix with samples of any advertising and promotional materials in original language that incorporate the Zogenix Trademarks prior to distributing such materials for use. Vendor shall comply with reasonable policies provided by Zogenix from time to time to maintain the goodwill and value of the Zogenix Trademarks. Distributor shall not, and shall cause its Affiliates not to, (i) use, seek to register, or otherwise claim rights in any Trademark that is confusingly similar to, misleading or deceptive with respect to, or that materially dilutes, any of the Zogenix Trademarks, or (ii) knowingly do, cause to be done, or knowingly omit to do any act, the doing, causing or omitting of which endangers, undermines, impairs, destroys or similarly affects, in any material respect, the validity or strength of any of the Zogenix Trademarks (including any registration or pending registration application relating thereto) or the value of the goodwill pertaining to any of the Zogenix Trademarks.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the 'Distributor' is responsible for complying with policies to maintain the goodwill and value of Zogenix Trademarks. However, the changed text replaces 'Distributor' with 'Vendor' in Section 9.7. The contradicted text contains the sentence 'Vendor shall comply with reasonable policies provided by Zogenix from time to time to maintain the goodwill and value of the Zogenix Trademarks.' which is now in conflict with previous and other uses of the word Distributor in other sections of the contract. This change creates ambiguity and uncertainty about which party, 'Distributor' or 'Vendor,' is responsible for these trademark-related obligations, creating a direct contradiction and making it unclear who is responsible.
